content.text: Patterns load like guns. In this world, that has never been a metaphor.

The Channeler is the caster, and casting is two acts. Weaving is the expensive one — the roll, the heat, the risk — and it banks a pattern as stored rounds. Firing spends a round as a cheap, clean action. One cast goes a long way; the burn happens once.
This page is the tree. The casting grammar lives on Weave & Fire, the pattern catalog and licensing on Patterns, and the bill — Charge now, Saturation forever — on Charge & Saturation.

content.stat_lean: Interface — it aims the Weave, the trigger, and sizes the magazine. Grit sizes the free vent that keeps the bill survivable.
content.theme: The licensed gun: weave-then-fire, banked rounds, corporate IP in the chamber.
content.design: No stat ceiling — the ceiling is structural: the only renewable weapon pool in the game.

content.text: Arrive loaded. Rounds persist until fired, so a working Channeler weaves in downtime and at rest nodes and walks into the mission at capacity. Weaving mid-fight is hot work — costlier in Charge, riskier the hotter you already run — so the tree's worst turn is a choice, never bookkeeping: run dry, or weave under fire.

Ordnance makes the gun bigger: deeper magazines, hotter loads, crit beats, siege work. Current makes the bill smaller: better venting, cooler weaves, and the squad-battery trick of loaning a round to someone else's gear.

The creditor texture: the Channeler pays two creditors at corporate rates (see The Creditor Table) — the armorer's bill in licenses and rounds, and the doc's bill in detox. Power now; the body invoices later.

content.label: COUNTERPLAY
items: The bill, twice — every weave is Charge, every license is credits, and both compound.
Capacity — the magazine has a ceiling, and a long fight outlasts a short loadout.
Theaters without prep — no rest nodes, no downtime, no reload logistics: a Channeler who couldn't prepare is a contractor holding an empty gun.

content.text: Pattern licenses are non-transferable and survive neither resale nor death. Rounds woven under an expired license are classified as counterfeit ordnance. Disposal teams bill the estate.
Tier chassis, requirements, and the install ladder live on Trees Overview. Install costs below are per augment at that tier.

augments.name: Rift Conduit
augments.effect: Unlocks Weaving and the pattern catalog: you can bank rounds and fire them. The Weave and Fire procedure and your round capacity — see Weave & Fire. The patterns themselves — see Patterns. The hardware that makes you a magazine.

augments.name: Grounding Mesh (Current)
augments.effect: Your free vent at encounter's end is Grit bonus +1.

augments.name: Field Loom (Current)
augments.effect: Weaving at rest nodes rolls with advantage.

augments.name: Snap Fire (Ordnance)
augments.effect: Once per turn, firing a Tier-1 pattern costs −1 AP (minimum 1).

augments.name: Overpressure (Ordnance)
augments.effect: Trade-off. When you Weave, you may load the rounds hot: they deal +1 damage die when fired, and you take +1 Charge at weave time per round loaded.

augments.name: Coolant Bleed (Current)
augments.effect: Once per fight, 1 AP: shed 2 Charge. It steams.

augments.name: Lending Coil (Current)
augments.effect: Weave a round into an ally's gear; they fire it with their own AP. One lent round at a time. The squad battery.

augments.name: Resonant Rounds (Ordnance)
augments.effect: Your targeted patterns gain +1 crit beat.

augments.name: Siege Lattice (Ordnance)
augments.effect: Shatter-class patterns count punch-through triple against Armor HP.

augments.name: Stable Loom (Current)
augments.effect: Your in-combat Weaves don't take the +1 hot-work Charge.

augments.name: Storm Sink (Current)
augments.effect: Once per fight, when a pattern or Charge effect hits you, halve it and gain 1 Charge.

augments.name: Barrage Protocol (Ordnance capstone)
augments.effect: Capstone. Once per fight, fire two different patterns as a single 4 AP action.

augments.name: Living Current (Current capstone)
augments.effect: Capstone. Your free vent doubles, and once per mission convert up to 3 Charge into rounds of a Tier-1 pattern you know. Heat into ammunition — the cycle, closed.

content.text: Thank you for choosing licensed weaving. Your conduit telemetry has been received. Your Saturation projection is available on request, and will be either way.
